title: Resetting the index
creator: mario64
content: Sometimes the index becomes too large, making searching very difficult. The solution is to reset the index, which involves deleting the global-messages-db.sqlite file. After Thunderbird rebuilds the index, searching is simpler and faster. Adding a menu or an automatic database reindexing process would be a significant improvement!

title: Thunderbird all of the sudden will not accept photos or document attachments to send.
creator: djb-sab
content:
T-Bird,

Using the latest version of Thunderbird, all of the sudden I cannot attach/send photos or documents. The 'send to' mail icon in the menu is not lit up to accept the attachment. The message come back 'no file associated'. My defaults are as they were. I have been using Thunderbird email client for likely twenty years. What has changed and how can I get my Windows 11 to recognize again? Would reloading the program help? Any information/help would be much appreciated as I am not a system dive in file changing techie. Thanks, Doug

title: Suggest an update for folder compaction
creator: atsdon1
content:
I don't know where this should go so if you can foward it on, please do so.

When I delete an email, a pop up gives me a chance to compact folders now, or remind me later. This is a minor annoyance and can probably use a fix. I do want to compact folders, but not while I'm in the middle of reading and deleting my email.

How about using whatever trigger is used to, instead of popping up the small window, do a delay and open the popup when it's more convenient and useful, like when I delete my trash or go to close the program?

title: Copying a thunderbird program from an old computer to a new computer
creator: VictorO
content: I have followed your instructions at https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer and gotten nowhere all day. on it. You have "Profile Folders", a folder called "Profiles", a bunch of profiles (folders) inside the Profiles folder and profiles in Roaming and in Local paths. When I get the old profile 'k41******' copied to the new computer in both the Roaming folder and the Local folder the Thunderbird program still will not show me the old files. I don't need any of the thousands of emails in the old computer, just the Templates in one account. This should be easy. I should just copy the profile k41***** ect. and paste it into the "Profiles" (notice the pleural there) and then Thunderbird should work. But it does not.

title: EMail is not being sent (VPN?). I've tried everything (((
creator: valeriya.mazepova
content:
Hello everyone Gmail is configured in Thunderbird and has always worked fine. The settings are exactly correct. At some point, the emails stopped being sent. I guess it's because of the VPN I used (not sure)

I receive incoming messages. And when trying to send an email:

"The message could not be sent because the connection to the Mail Server (SMTP) could not be established "smtp.gmail.com ". The server may be unavailable or refuse to connect via SMTP".

I've already deleted all the VPNs. Reinstalled Thunderbird. Cleaned PC with CCleaner. Turned off the firewall. Tried another wifi. Logged in with a password for Google apps. Tried disabling the proxy in Thunderbird. Changed 465/587 ports. The latest version of Thunderbird.

The mail is sent in the browser on Google's website. I tried to install Betterbird, the error is the same! The problem is the same on other computers too. I tried adding another gmail, but it's not being sent either. Just in case, the country is Russia.

Please help me, what can I do?

P.s. Sorry for the mistakes, English is not my native language.
